userStories.title: Project scaffolding and CLI entry point
userStories.description: Initialize npm package with TypeScript, Ink v6, tsup. Create CLI entry point that runs with `npx agentscout`. Verify `npm run build` succeeds and the bin script executes.
testingProcedure: Run `npm run build` — should compile without errors
Run `node dist/cli.js` — should show a placeholder message

userStories.title: Session scanner discovers and parses Claude Code sessions
userStories.description: Scan ~/.claude/projects/ to discover all projects and sessions. Parse sessions-index.json for metadata. Parse JSONL session logs to extract tool call patterns, Bash commands, user messages, and error counts.
testingProcedure: Run `node dist/cli.js` — should discover projects in ~/.claude/
Should output project count and session count
acceptanceCriteria: Parses JSONL to extract tool_use calls and Bash commands

userStories.title: Pattern detection identifies manual work
userStories.description: Analyze extracted tool calls and Bash commands to identify patterns of manual work. Detect: frequent git commands via Bash, database queries, manual file management, API calls via curl, deployment commands, etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Groups commands by category (git, db, deploy, etc.)

userStories.title: Curated tool catalog with pattern matching
userStories.description: Create a curated JSON catalog of ~20-30 tools (MCP servers, CLI tools, packages) with pattern signatures. Matcher maps detected patterns to relevant tools. Each tool has metadata for all 3 scoring pillars.
testingProcedure: Matcher should return relevant tools for detected patterns
Each tool should have all required metadata fields
acceptanceCriteria: Each entry has: name, description, install command, pattern triggers, scoring metadata

userStories.title: 3-pillar scoring engine (Low/Med/High)
userStories.description: Score each matched tool on Workflow Ownership, Pain Eliminated, and Agent Readiness. Scores are Low/Med/High based on tool metadata + detected usage patterns. Rank recommendations by combined score.
acceptanceCriteria: Scores reflect actual user patterns where possible

userStories.title: AI-powered sell descriptions via claude -p
userStories.description: Shell out to `claude -p` to generate compelling, pain-focused descriptions for each recommendation. Batch into a single prompt. Fallback to template descriptions if claude CLI unavailable.
testingProcedure: Run with claude CLI available — should generate custom descriptions
Run without claude CLI — should use fallback templates
acceptanceCriteria: Descriptions focus on human suffering eliminated

userStories.title: Rich terminal report with Ink
userStories.description: Build beautiful terminal output using Ink v6: header with scan stats, recommendation cards with Low/Med/High score bars, sell descriptions, and install commands. Show analysis spinner during scan.
testingProcedure: Run `npx agentscout` — should show formatted report
Report should have colored scores and clean layout
acceptanceCriteria: Each recommendation is a distinct card
Install command shown for each tool

userStories.title: End-to-end polish and npx readiness
userStories.description: Polish the full flow: spinner → scan → analyze → describe → report. Handle edge cases (no sessions, claude CLI missing, empty patterns). Verify npx execution works.
testingProcedure: Run `npx agentscout` from scratch — full flow works
Run with no ~/.claude/ — shows helpful message
acceptanceCriteria: Full flow completes in under 60 seconds
npx agentscout works after npm pack

tigers: Session log format may change across Claude Code versions
Large session directories may be slow to scan
Curated tool database can go stale
elephants: Privacy perception of scanning session logs
Curated recommendations may feel generic if pattern matching is too simple
Convincing the human is the actual product challenge
mitigations: Batch AI analysis into single prompt, show spinner
Sample last 30 days by default, use index files
Supplement curated list with live MCP registry queries
Everything runs locally, be explicit about privacy
Focus on high-signal patterns, quality over quantity
AI-generated descriptions are the key differentiator
